name: Bellús Reservoir Loop from Xàtiva
name_original: El Pantano de Bellus — Circular desde Xàtiva
description: This difficult 40.2-mile road cycling route from Xàtiva circles the scenic Bellus Reservoir, gaining 3226 feet of elevation.
region_intro: Road cycling around Xàtiva offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain, situated in the province of Valencia. The region is characterized by high mountains to the south and significant hills to its east and west, providing a range of routes from easy, paved paths to challenging rides with considerable elevation gain. The area's topography, nestled between the Albaida and Cànyoles river basins, includes natural features such as the Albaida River and the Bellús Reservoir. This varied environment ensures a wide array of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Xàtiva The most popular road cycling route is Bellús Reservoir Loop from Xàtiva, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail that takes about 4 hours to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the scenic reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Xàtiva Cathedral – Bellús Reservoir loop from Xàtiva, a difficult 55.3 miles (88.9 km) path. This route combines historical landmarks with extensive distances and substantial climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Xàtiva Cathedral – Tous Dam loop from Xàtiva, a 42.2 miles (67.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Xàtiva is defined by its mountainous backdrop, river valleys, and historical landmarks like Xàtiva Castle and the Cova Negra Municipal Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Xàtiva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Xàtiva?
A: Road cycling around Xàtiva offers a diverse landscape, ranging from mostly paved paths with gentle gradients to challenging ascents through high mountains and significant hills. The region, nestled between the Albaida and Cànyoles river basins, features varied topography, including river valleys and mountainous backdrops, ensuring a wide array of cycling experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Xàtiva?
A: Yes, Xàtiva offers several road cycling routes suitable for beginners. There are 7 easy routes available, often featuring mostly paved surfaces and less elevation gain. An example is the Manuel–Pobla Llarga Greenway – Albereda Jaume I loop, which is great for any fitness level and typically takes up to 2 hours with around 500 ft of elevation.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Xàtiva?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Xàtiva provides 6 difficult road cycling routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853328951/bellus-reservoir-loop-from-xativa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Reservoir Loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, covering 64.7 km with nearly 1,000 meters of ascent. Other demanding routes include the Molí Guarner – Cova Negra loop and the Gorgo Catalán – Gorgo de la Escalera loop, which may even require pushing your bike in some segments.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while road cycling in Xàtiva?
A: Many routes in Xàtiva offer views of historical landmarks. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2101633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;, perched on the Sierra del Castell, provides breathtaking panoramic views and can be admired from various cycling paths. Some trails also explore historical aqueducts, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt;, known for its crystal-clear pools, is a notable natural canyon. The Cova Negra Municipal Natural Park, situated along the Albaida River, offers lush landscapes and biodiversity. You can also find scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de Chella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Xàtiva?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Xàtiva are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1673712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xàtiva Cathedral – Bellús Reservoir loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17308280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xàtiva Cathedral – Tous Dam loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;. The Molí Guarner – Cova Negra loop and the Public washhouse and Sants River – Fountain of the 25 Spouts loop are also popular circular options.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Xàtiva?
A: The road cycling routes in Xàtiva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of challenging climbs and scenic descents, and the opportunity to explore both historical landmarks and natural parks like Cova Negra.

Q: Can I find routes that follow rivers or reservoirs in Xàtiva?
A: Yes, the region's topography includes the Albaida River and the Bellús Reservoir, which are often incorporated into cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Albaida River – Bellús Reservoir loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a moderate ride that combines both natural features.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that connect Xàtiva to neighboring towns?
A: Yes, Xàtiva serves as an excellent base for longer road cycling journeys to nearby towns. You can embark on extensive rides to destinations such as Alcoy (64.6 km with 1,774 m ascent), Villalonga (61.4 km with 1,737 m ascent), Biar (69.2 km with 1,578 m ascent), and even Javea (101.2 km with 637 m ascent), showcasing the extensive network of cycling possibilities in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Xàtiva?
A: Xàtiva's Mediterranean climate generally makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are mild, allowing for cycling with appropriate layering.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Xàtiva on komoot?
A: There are 28 road cycling routes available around Xàtiva on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 7 easy, 15 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for almost every cyclist.
tips: Good asphalt, great views but quite a lot of traffic for Spanish countryside.

One of the best preserved monasteries in the Valencian Community. It is part of the long-distance route GR-236 known as the Route of the Monasteries. A site very rich in its history and significance in the area that make it worth visiting.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging road cycling route requiring a very good level of fitness. It's classified as a "Hard road ride" due to its considerable distance of approximately 64.7 kilometers and a significant elevation gain of around 980 meters.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces that are generally easy to ride. However, be prepared for potentially mixed road conditions, as some segments may be unpaved and more difficult.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take around 4 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Xàtiva. While specific parking details aren't provided, Xàtiva, as a starting point, typically offers various parking options within the town or near its outskirts.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or registration to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or registrations are required for cycling this route. However, always ensure your bicycle complies with general road safety regulations in Spain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it is generally not suitable for cycling with pets due to the nature of road cycling and potential traffic.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valencia region generally offers good cycling weather for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures than the heat of summer. Always check the local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views, with the Bellús Reservoir and spillway being a central scenic highlight. You'll also pass by the Convent of Corpus Christi and enjoy the natural landscapes surrounding the reservoir and the broader Xàtiva area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of La Defensa del Sur and El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or rest stops along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a demanding ride, starting and ending in Xàtiva means you'll have access to cafes, restaurants, and amenities in the town. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring appropriate road cycling gear, including a well-maintained road bike, helmet, sufficient water, energy snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Layered clothing is also recommended to adapt to changing temperatures.
